conflict_decisions.replacement_design: registered descriptor -> Node strict adapter -> optional closed proposal-only Python worker -> Node schema/provenance revalidation -> single transaction candidate; raw prompt/SKILL has no DB, Issue, PLAN, gate, command, network, credential, repository or state authority
conflict_decisions.positive_oracle: allowlisted descriptor and sealed entry/input digests produce a versioned proposal; repeated input is byte-identical; Node revalidation receipt exists; no authoritative state changes before commit gate
conflict_decisions.negative_oracle: raw prompt/SKILL invocation, unregistered descriptor, direct write/command/SQL/path/code output, credential/repository/.helix input, self-approval or direct state mutation is rejected before side effect

conflict_decisions.replacement_design: workflow_model_proposal.v1 -> workflow_completeness_receipt.v1 -> derived_requirements_proposal.v1 -> workflow_derivation_bundle.v1; every stage references predecessor digest and no single JSON object is required to validate against two incompatible root schemas
conflict_decisions.positive_oracle: each envelope validates only its named canonical schema, predecessor digests match, completeness is green before derivation, and bundle has exact transition-to-requirement/AC/test/surface joins
conflict_decisions.negative_oracle: single root claims simultaneous workflow-model and derived-requirements schema conformance, derived stage precedes completeness, stage digest mismatches, or unresolved items are silently omitted

conflict_decisions.replacement_design: one versioned canonical schema per workflow, requirement, derived-surface and test family; all 12 prose requirement fields are represented, with typed empty/N-A disposition rather than field deletion; prose clauses become field-constraint edges; legacy names are input-only aliases with expiry and migration receipts
conflict_decisions.positive_oracle: schema/prose field denominator and required/applicability matrix agree; examples validate; every clause reaches one field constraint and every required field reaches an assertion
conflict_decisions.negative_oracle: additionalProperties drift, prose-required field absent from schema, untyped object item, silent legacy alias acceptance, or field existence used as behavior completion

conflict_decisions.replacement_design: seal original approval fixture as expected-unreachable negative; positive fixture adds manager/executive approval transitions to APPROVED and authorized cancellation transitions from every reachable nonterminal state to CANCELLED; positive and negative use distinct IDs and digests
conflict_decisions.positive_oracle: graph traversal from initial_state reaches every declared success/cancel terminal, no terminal has outgoing state mutation, and each added transition satisfies trigger/action/next-state/actor/data/permission constraints
conflict_decisions.negative_oracle: original digest deterministically reports APPROVED and CANCELLED unreachable and cannot be counted as positive coverage

conflict_decisions.replacement_design: canonical names candidates, destinations and deadline_policy; candidate_set/destination candidates/deadline handling are expiring input aliases. Add schedule_id/task_id/ready_at/deadline/dependencies/order/preemption/time basis, resource capability/capacity/concurrency/cost/budget/recovery, allocation objective/priority/fairness/reallocation/degradation/fallback/dead-letter and bounded terminal route
conflict_decisions.positive_oracle: all ready tasks receive a deterministic schedule and capability/capacity-valid allocation or explicit bounded checkpoint/fallback/dead-letter; resource exhaustion never drops required work; canonical output contains no legacy names
conflict_decisions.negative_oracle: missing schedule entity, alias in canonical output, capacity over-allocation, undefined deadline/preemption/fairness, unbounded retry, absent fallback/dead-letter, or required processing dropped
